decisions.decision: Switched markup-tool click suppression from sticky-flag pattern to delta-at-click-time
decisions.rationale: The sticky-flag (markupDraggedRef set true on >4px max travel during the hold) was asking the wrong question. A fast Count click routinely moves 5-15 screen pixels mid-flight even when the pointer returns near the press point by mouseup — those legitimate clicks were being suppressed. Delta-at-click-time compares only the FINAL mousedown→mouseup displacement, so mid-flight wobble is irrelevant. Mirrors CAD tool conventions (Illustrator, Figma, SketchUp). Code is also smaller — net deletion vs 665835f.

decisions.decision: Phase 09 closure landed BEFORE the rapid-click fix was discovered
decisions.rationale: The rapid-click drop was caused by 665835f (the UX follow-up to Test 11) and only surfaced after Phase 09 closure. Decision: do NOT reopen Phase 09 — the fix is a follow-up quick task (260518-rcp), recorded in STATE.md's Quick Tasks table, with the debug session resolved. The phase's 12/12 UAT PASS record stands because Test 11 itself does pass (LMB no longer pans during markup); the rapid-click issue is a new defect class introduced by the fix, not a regression of the original UAT scenario.
